reviews.review_msg: Overall, it was a good class. Prof is quite rude. Quizzes and discussions are marked strictly, and even minor details will cost you marks. I once cited a range of pages (e.g., 251–253) instead of a single page and lost two points, despite the rubric not specifying this requirement lol.

Clear marking scheme for assignments/presentations. Very responsive to emails. There is a strong emphasis on class participation which makes up some of your marks. He likes to bring in guest speakers which I found interesting and unique from other profs. The workload was heavy but it was alright because he and his TA are generous markers.
she shouldnt be a teacher . she cant write and honestly i dont know anyone who actually liked her
It was just embarrassing watching her fumble and stutter for 2 hours each lecture. No wonder on 7 of 80 kids would show up by the term's end LMAO

I really liked this course! Most issues came from the fact that there wasn't enough enrollment to have a TA so that meant lots of group projects. Sophie is very nice and knowledgeable but very serious, not necessarily one of those profs you joke around with.

I found this prof to be a little difficult... Because he works for the government, I felt like he had a preconceived notion and a very specific mindset on what ecology and conservation is, and how natural resources should be managed. I would recommend taking his courses in senior years; his teaching and writing is very advanced for undergraduates.
